title: triclisia angolensis
intro: Triclisia angolensis, also known as the African soap bark tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Annonaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Triclisia angolensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as having potential anti-cancer and anti-malarial properties. Triclisia angolensis can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional soap-making due to its bark's soap-like properties.
